reasoning:
Based on the climatology of Ajaccio, Corsica, the daily mean temperature steadily decreases from November into early February due to seasonal cooling. The reference date is November 9. Without exact forecast data for these future dates (since they are beyond a reliable medium-range forecast relative to the knowledge cutoff), we evaluate the likelihood that subsequent dates will be warmer than November 9 by looking at the expected climatological difference and the typical standard deviation of temperatures. 

1. For Nov 16 (7 days later), the average temperature is about 1°C cooler than Nov 9. Given typical standard deviations, there is approximately a 35-40% chance it is warmer due to simple weather variance.
2. For Dec 9 (30 days later), the average daily temperature drops significantly (about 3-4°C cooler than Nov 9). The likelihood of a single day in December being warmer than a day in early November is roughly 14%.
3. For Feb 7 (90 days later), this falls in the coldest part of the year. The mean temperature is around 5°C colder than early November. The probability of an anomalous enough warm setup in February coupled with a cold setup on Nov 9 yields approx 8% chance.

While uncertainty often increases (bringing p closer to 0.5) for long-horizon forecasting of stationary series, for strongly seasonal variables like temperature, predictability of the directional difference can actually improve or result in a high-confidence skew (closer to 0) as the underlying mean moves further away from the origin point.
